rules:
Put a Single Strike Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Shuffle up to 2 Single Strike Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if it is Knocked Out), put an Energy attached to the Attacking Pokémon into your opponent's hand.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Search your deck for a Psychic Energy card or a Basic Psychic Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Put a Special Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon on the bottom of their deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Put a Basic Pokémon from your opponent's discard pile onto their Bench.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.
Many scientists suspect that this Pokémon originated outside the Galar region, based on the patterns on its body.
It stores an overflowing amount of electric energy inside its body. Even a small shock makes it explode.

rules:
Choose up to 2 of your Rapid Strike Pokémon and heal 60 damage from each of them.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Discard your hand and search your deck for up to 2 Trainer cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Put up to 3 Prize cards into your hand. Then, for each Prize card you put into your hand in this way, put a card from your hand face down as a Prize card.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Attach a Water Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon V. If you do, draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Choose 1 or both:
• Put up to 2 Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.
• Put up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

During this turn, your Single Strike Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Discard a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon, and discard a Stadium in play.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 2 cards. If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, draw 2 more cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Put any number of cards from your hand on the bottom of your deck in any order. Then, draw that many cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Rapid Strike Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 3 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, your opponent discards Pokémon from their Bench until they have 3.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Move up to 3 damage counters from your Active Pokémon to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

This card can only be attached to a Rapid Strike Pokémon. If this card is attached to anything other than a Rapid Strike Pokémon, discard this card.

As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. The Pokémon this card is attached to can't be Paralyzed, and if it is already Paralyzed, it recovers from that Special Condition.

As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy.

If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if it is Knocked Out), draw a card.

flavorText: Common throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon has strong teeth and can chew through the toughest of berry shells.

attacks.name: Scrounge
attacks.effect: Put a Pokémon Tool card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: It eats berries nonstop—a habit that has made it more resilient than it looks. It'll show up on farms, searching for yet more berries.

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Fur Attack
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.

attacks.effect: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 120 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium.

attacks.name: Sky Return
attacks.effect: Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.

flavorText: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.
Its color changes for concealment and also when its mood or health changes. The darker the color, the healthier it is.

attacks.name: Weather Force
attacks.effect: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

flavorText: Its form changes depending on the weather. The rougher conditions get, the rougher Castform's disposition!

attacks.name: Gale Claws
attacks.effect: If you played a Rapid Strike Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack also does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its fur would all stand on end if it smelled a Seviper nearby. Its sharp claws tear up its foes.

attacks.name: Blissful Blast
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you did any damage with this attack, you may attach up to 3 Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Superbeam
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: A faulty update was added to its programming. Its behavior is noticeably strange, so the experiment may have been a failure.

attacks.name: Tri Attack
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Even though it doesn't die in the vacuum of space, it can't move around very well in zero gravity.
It was built 20 years ago by scientists who dreamed of exploring space. Their dreams have yet to come true.

attacks.name: Raging Bull
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon. This Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: They live in groups. The one with the longest, thickest, and most-scarred horns is the boss of the herd.

attacks.name: Metal Slash
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It has a body and heart of steel. It worked with its allies to punish people when they hurt Pokémon.

attacks.name: Zap Traction
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Max Rush
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Max Rush attack does 150 more damage.

attacks.name: Seismic Rift
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: While seeking iron for food, it digs tunnels by breaking through bedrock with its steel horns.
It habitually shows off its strength with the size of sparks it creates by ramming its steel body into boulders.
When it evolves, it sheds the steel carapace that covered its whole body and develops a new one.

attacks.name: Field Crush
attacks.effect: If your opponent has a Stadium in play, discard it.

attacks.name: Fists of Strife
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: Inhabiting the mountains of a distant region, this Pokémon races across sheer cliffs, training its legs and refining its moves.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: With quick movements, it chases down its foes, attacking relentlessly with its horns until it prevails.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It is usually motionless, but when attacked, it rotates at high speed and then crashes into its opponent.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its bite injects a potent poison, enough to paralyze large bird Pokémon that try to prey on it.

attacks.name: Shadow Ripper
attacks.effect: You may put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Ghostly Cries
attacks.effect: For each Pokémon in your opponent's discard pile, put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like. If you placed any damage counters in this way, your opponent shuffles all Pokémon from their discard pile into their deck.

flavorText: It was bound to a fissure in an Odd Keystone as punishment for misdeeds 500 years ago.

attacks.name: Strong Tail
attacks.effect: If you played a Single Strike Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Constant polishing makes the edge of the blade on its tail extremely sharp. It's Zangoose's archrival.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The small spikes covering its body developed from scales. They inject a toxin that causes fainting.

attacks.name: Max Toxify
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 12 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Concoction
attacks.effect: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Word of Ruin
attacks.effect: At the end of your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon will be Knocked Out.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: While chanting strange spells, this Pokémon combines its internal toxins with what it's eaten, creating strange potions.

attacks.name: Aura Burn
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Long ago, during a time when droves of factories fouled the air with pollution, Weezing changed into this form for some reason.

attacks.name: Mixin' Toxin
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. Attach a Darkness Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Smog Burst
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Darkness Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Very rarely, a sudden mutation can result in two small Koffing twins becoming conjoined as a Weezing.

attacks.name: Smog
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It adores polluted air. Some claim that Koffing used to be more plentiful in the Galar region than they are now.

attacks.name: Training
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: If Kubfu pulls the long white hair on its head, its fighting spirit heightens and power wells up from the depths of its belly.

attacks.name: Synchro Buster
attacks.effect: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Searching for an opponent to test its skills against, it emerges onto land. Once the battle is over, it returns to the sea.
Its tentacles tear off easily, but it isn't alarmed when that happens—it knows they'll grow back. It's about as smart as a three-year-old.

attacks.name: Sand Pulse
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: G-Max Cyclone
attacks.effect: Move any amount of Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.
V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fling
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Passimian live in groups of about 20, with each member performing an assigned role. Through cooperation, the group survives.

attacks.name: Rogue Fangs
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each Single Strike Pokémon in your discard pile.

flavorText: They live alone without forming packs. They will only listen to orders from Trainers who can draw out their true power.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: When it rubs the rocks on its neck against you, that's proof of its love for you. However, the rocks are sharp, so the gesture is quite painful!

attacks.name: Double Lariat
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Before it stops to think, it just starts pummeling. There are records of its turning back avalanches with a flurry of punches.

attacks.name: Double Lariat
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Crabrawler has been known to mistake Exeggutor for a coconut tree and climb it. The enraged Exeggutor shakes it off and stomps it.

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Never touch its shadowlike body, or you'll be shown the horrific memories behind the picture carved into it.

attacks.name: Brutal Swing
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It's said that this Pokémon was formed when an ancient clay tablet was drawn to a vengeful spirit.

attacks.name: Feint
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Dynablade
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage for each of your opponent's Pokémon V in play.

flavorText: Sharply attuned to others' wishes for help, this Pokémon seeks out those in need and aids them in battle.

attacks.name: Thunderous Kick
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Leek Strike
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 90 more damage, and this attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: After deflecting attacks with its hard leaf shield, it strikes back with its sharp leek stalk. The leek stalk is both weapon and food.

attacks.name: Puncture
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: The stalks of leeks are thicker and longer in the Galar region. Farfetch'd that adapted to these stalks took on a unique form.

attacks.name: Triple Heads
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads. If all of them are heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: These Diglett triplets dig over 60 miles below sea level. No one knows what it's like underground.

attacks.name: Dig
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It burrows through the ground at a shallow depth. It leaves raised earth in its wake, making it easy to spot.

fullDisplayName: Shadow Rider Calyrex VMAX 075/198 Chilling Reign

attacks.name: Max Geist
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

fullDisplayName: Shadow Rider Calyrex V 074/198 Chilling Reign

attacks.name: Shadow Mist
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Special Energy or Stadium cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Astral Barrage
attacks.effect: Choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon and put 5 damage counters on each of them.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: If you're too loud around it, you risk being torn apart by the claws on its tentacle. This Pokémon is also known as the Forest Witch.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Using the braids on its head, it pummels foes to get them to quiet down. One blow from those braids would knock out a professional boxer.
If this Pokémon senses a strong emotion, it will run away as fast as it can. It prefers areas without people.

attacks.name: Rapid Strike Tentacles
attacks.effect: Reveal any number of Rapid Strike cards from your hand. This attack does 40 damage for each card you revealed in this way. Then, shuffle those cards into your deck.

flavorText: It's said that Malamar's hypnotic powers played a role in certain history-changing events.
By exposing foes to the blinking of its luminescent spots, Inkay demoralizes them, and then it seizes the chance to flee.

attacks.name: Follow the Scent
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. Put a number of cards up to the number of heads from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Slurpuff's fur contains a lot of air, making it soft to the touch and lighter than it looks.

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The sweet smell of cotton candy perfumes Swirlix's fluffy fur. This Pokémon spits out sticky string to tangle up its enemies.

attacks.name: Reinforced Punch
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Megaton Fall
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: There's a theory that inside Golurk is a perpetual motion machine that produces limitless energy, but this belief hasn't been proven.
This Pokémon was created from clay. It received orders from its master many thousands of years ago, and it still follows those orders to this day.

attacks.name: Crescent Glow
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Psychic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. If you go second and it's your first turn, instead search for up to 3 Psychic Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Photon Laser
attacks.effect: If you have at least 5 Energy in play, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Those who sleep holding Cresselia's feather are assured of joyful dreams. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

attacks.name: Resolute Spite
attacks.effect: Put up to 7 damage counters on this Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter you placed in this way.

attacks.name: Eerie Light
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Resentment at being cast off made it spring into being. Some say that treating it well will satisfy it, and it will once more become a stuffed toy.
There's a proverb that says, "Shun the house where Shuppet gather in the growing dusk."

attacks.name: Brainwave
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: To protect its Trainer, it will expend all its psychic power to create a small black hole.

attacks.name: Mirage Step
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Kirlia and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: If its horns capture the warm feelings of people or Pokémon, its body warms up slightly.

attacks.name: Psyray
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Pain Burst
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said to emerge from darkness to steal the lives of those who become lost in mountains.
If you get the feeling of being watched in darkness when nobody is around, Haunter is there.

attacks.name: Sleep Pulse
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: With its gas-like body, it can sneak into any place it desires. However, it can be blown away by wind.
Because Galarian Slowpoke eat the seeds of a plant that grows only in Galar, their tails have developed a spicy flavor.

attacks.name: Cross Fist
attacks.effect: If 1 of your other Rapid Strike Pokémon used an attack during your last turn, this attack also does 160 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Assisting Spark
attacks.effect: You may attach a Lightning Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: As it flies around, it shoots lightning all over the place and causes forest fires. It is therefore disliked.

attacks.name: Coordinated Bolt
attacks.effect: If 1 of your other Rapid Strike Pokémon used an attack during your last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Spark Rush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.

flavorText: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

attacks.name: Thunder Spear
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Searchlight Tail
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. If you find any Energy cards there, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Its tail shines bright and strong. It has been prized since long ago as a beacon for sailors.
It stores electricity in its fluffy fleece. If it stores up too much, it will start to go bald in those patches.

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Rubbing its fleece generates electricity. You'll want to pet it because it's cute, but if you use your bare hand, you'll get a painful shock.

fullDisplayName: Ice Rider Calyrex VMAX 046/198 Chilling Reign

attacks.name: Ride of the High King
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Lance
attacks.effect: You may discard up to 2 Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 120 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

fullDisplayName: Ice Rider Calyrex V 045/198 Chilling Reign

attacks.name: Glacial Lance
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rapid-Fisted Rush
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Rapid Strike Pokémon in play.

flavorText: This form of Urshifu is a strong believer in defeating foes by raining many blows down on them. Its strikes are nonstop, flowing like a river.
Its nictitating membranes let it pick out foes' weak points so it can precisely blast them with water that shoots from its fingertips at Mach 3.

attacks.name: Bounce
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Highly intelligent but also very lazy, it keeps enemies out of its territory by laying traps everywhere.

attacks.name: Keep Calling
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Rapid Strike Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: When it gets wet, its skin changes color, and this Pokémon becomes invisible as if it were camouflaged.

attacks.name: Smash Turn
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ocean Loop
attacks.effect: Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: Although it's called a guardian deity, terrible calamities sometimes befall those who recklessly approach Tapu Fini.

attacks.name: Hail Prison
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It shatters drift ice with its strong tusks. Its thick layer of blubber repels enemy attacks.
Be it Spheal or a Poké Ball, it will spin any round object on its nose with the greatest of ease.
Its body is covered in fluffy fur. The fur keeps it from feeling cold while it is rolling on ice.

attacks.name: Crystal Breath
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It spits out cold air of nearly −60 degrees Fahrenheit to freeze its quarry. It brings frozen prey back to its lair and neatly lines them up.
It can only survive in cold areas. It bounces happily around, even in environments as cold as −150 degrees Fahrenheit.

attacks.name: Frosty Typhoon
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Frosty Typhoon.

flavorText: This is Castform's form when caught in a hailstorm. Its whole body is chilled, and its skin is partially frozen!

attacks.name: Rainfall
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This is Castform's form when pelted by rain. In an experiment where it was placed in a shower, this Pokémon didn't change to this form.

attacks.name: Package Delivery
attacks.effect: Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck. If you do, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It has a generous habit of sharing its food with people and Pokémon, so it's always scrounging around for more food.

attacks.name: Two-Hit KO
attacks.effect: During your next turn, if the Defending Pokémon is damaged by an attack from a Rapid Strike Pokémon, it will be Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Nasty Plot
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: With its claws, it leaves behind signs for its friends to find. The number of distinct signs is said to be over 500.

attacks.name: Cut Down
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a cunning yet savage disposition. It waits for parents to leave their nests, and then it sneaks in to steal their eggs.

attacks.name: Wintry Call
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Melony cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Crossing icy seas is no issue for this cold-resistant Pokémon. Its smooth skin is a little cool to the touch.

attacks.name: Fireball Shot
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It's skilled at both offense and defense, and it gets pumped up when cheered on. But if it starts showboating, it could put itself in a tough spot.

attacks.name: Volley Kick
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It kicks berries right off the branches of trees and then juggles them with its feet, practicing its footwork.
It has special pads on the backs of its feet, and one on its nose. Once it's raring to fight, these pads radiate tremendous heat.

attacks.name: Dynamite Tackle
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has 10 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 150 more damage.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: According to legends, it was hatched from a flaming cocoon to save people and Pokémon that were suffering from the cold.

attacks.name: Flame Charge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It protects itself with flame. Long years ago, people believed Larvesta had a nest on the sun.

attacks.name: High-Pressure Blast
attacks.effect: Discard a Stadium in play. If you can't, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Castform changes to this form when it basks in bright sunlight. When you touch its glowing skin, it feels all dried out!

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Max Blaze
attacks.effect: Choose up to 2 of your Benched Rapid Strike Pokémon and attach an Energy card from your discard pile to each of them.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Pack Call
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Grass Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. If you go second and it's your first turn, search for up to 3 Grass Pokémon instead of 1. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Repeated Whip
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Once the vines on Zarude's body tear off, they become nutrients in the soil. This helps the plants of the forest grow.

attacks.name: Wood Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Raging Repeated Strike
attacks.effect: Discard any amount of Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 30 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: The one with the best drumming techniques becomes the boss of the troop. It has a gentle disposition and values harmony among its group.

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: When it's drumming out rapid beats in battle, it gets so caught up in the rhythm that it won't even notice that it's already knocked out its opponent.

attacks.name: Hit Twice
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It attacks with rapid beats of its stick. As it strikes with amazing speed, it gets more and more pumped.

attacks.name: Tread On
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: A kick from the hardened tips of this Pokémon's legs leaves a wound in the opponent's body and soul that will never heal.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Any Corvisquire that pecks at this Pokémon will be greeted with a smack from its sepals followed by a sharp kick.
When under attack, it secretes a sweet and delicious sweat. The scent only calls more enemies to it.

attacks.name: Winter Horn
attacks.effect: If you have a Stadium in play, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: They migrate according to the seasons. People can tell the season by looking at Sawsbuck's horns.

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The turning of the seasons changes the color and scent of this Pokémon's fur. People use it to mark the seasons.
This Pokémon is known to bring blizzards. A shake of its massive body is enough to cause whiteout conditions.
The berries that grow around its belly are like ice pops. Galarian Darumaka absolutely love these berries.

attacks.name: Max Plant
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Leaflet Dance
attacks.effect: Attach any number of Grass Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Slash Back
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Single-Horn Throw
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 160 more damage.

flavorText: It roams through forests searching for sweet nectar. Although it boasts fantastic physical strength, it's not that good at flying.

attacks.name: Rapid Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It flies through the night sky, sprinkling sparkly dust. According to some, if that dust sticks to you, good things will happen to you.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: These very cowardly Pokémon join together and use Reflect to protect their nest.

attacks.name: Persist Sting
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, it is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Jet Spear
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: May appear in a swarm. Flies at violent speeds, all the while stabbing with the toxic stinger on its rear.

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 40 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: While awaiting evolution, it hides from predators under leaves and in nooks of branches.
Its poison stinger is very powerful. Its bright-colored body is intended to warn off its enemies.
